title: Personio Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Personio** is a European HR platform exposing a v1 REST API at `https://api.personio.de/v1`. This connector — `PersonioConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "personio"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Personio account:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Auth | `/auth` | OAuth2 client-credentials → rotating bearer |
| Employees | `/company/employees` | List, get, create, update + custom-attributes |
| Attendances | `/company/attendances` | List + create time-tracking entries, update |
| Time-Offs | `/company/time-offs` + `/company/time-off-types` | Book absences, list absence types |
| Documents | `/company/document-categories` + `/company/employees/{id}/documents` | List categories, multipart upload |
| Departments / Offices | `/company/departments` + `/company/offices` | Org-structure read |
| Projects | `/company/projects` | Project list |
| Custom Attributes | `/company/employees/custom-attributes` | Schema introspection |
| Recruitment | `/recruiting/applications` + `/recruiting/applicants/{id}` | List ATS applications, fetch applicant detail |

The connector normalises **employees** into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), honours `Retry-After`, and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::PersonioHTTPClient`).

### Token rotation (READ THIS)

Personio rotates the bearer on **every successful response**. The HTTP client reads the `Authorization` response header, overwrites its cached token, and fans the new value out to `PersonioConnector._persist_rotated_token` so `BaseConnector.set_token()` persists it. Re-using a prior token typically returns 401. On a 401 mid-call the client purges the cache, re-runs `/auth`, and retries the original request once.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate Personio API credentials

1. Sign in to https://app.personio.com.
2. Open **Settings → Integrations → API Credentials**.
3. Click **Generate new credential**, name it (e.g. `Shielva`), and grant the scopes you need:
   - Employees: **read** (+ **write** for create/update),
   - Attendances / Time-Offs: **read** (+ **write** for create),
   - Documents: **read** (+ **write** for upload),
   - Recruiting: **read**.
4. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** — the secret is shown ONCE; if you lose it you must regenerate.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Personio**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `client_id` (string) | Client ID from step 4 |
| `client_secret` (secret) | Client Secret from step 4 |
| `partner_id` (string, optional) | `SHIELVA` (default) |
| `app_id` (string, optional) | `shielva-connector` (default) |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | `https://api.personio.de/v1` (default) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | `60` (default) |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates the two secrets, persists config, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, PENDING)`. It does NOT call Personio.

### 3. Authenticate

The gateway calls `authenticate()` (or the equivalent `authorize()`) which `POST`s to `/auth`, captures the bearer from the `Authorization` response header, persists it via `set_token()`, and returns `TokenInfo`.

### 4. First health check

`health_check()` lists 1 employee as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED` (an auto-reauth retry already happened). On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Personio v1 uses an **OAuth2 client-credentials** grant but advertises itself as `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"` because the user-facing install fields are just two pre-shared secrets (`client_id` + `client_secret`). No browser dance, no PKCE.

### Credential model

- `client_id` — Personio API Client ID.
- `client_secret` — paired secret; encrypted at rest by the platform secret store.
- `partner_id` (optional) — sent as the `X-Personio-Partner-ID` header (default `SHIELVA`).
- `app_id` (optional) — sent as the `X-Personio-App-ID` header (default `shielva-connector`).

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `client_id` + `client_secret`. Pushes them into `PersonioHTTPClient`. Does **not** call Personio. |
| `authorize(...)` | Delegates to `authenticate()` — the OAuth2 path on the gateway maps to client-credentials here. |
| `authenticate()` | `POST /auth?client_id=…&client_secret=…`. Captures the bearer from the `Authorization` response header (fallback: `data.token` in the JSON body). Persists via `set_token()`. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /company/employees?limit=1` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | First request triggers `authenticate()` under an `asyncio.Lock` to avoid stampedes. |
| **token rotation** | Every successful response carries a NEW `Authorization` header. The client overwrites its cache + persists via `_persist_rotated_token → set_token()`. |
| 401 recovery | One automatic re-auth + retry. A second 401 raises `PersonioAuthError`. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request (after `/auth`):

```
Authorization: Bearer <jwt>            ← rotated; updated from each response
Accept:        application/json
X-Personio-Partner-ID: SHIELVA
X-Personio-App-ID:     shielva-connector
```

### Why the rotation matters

If the rotated header is ignored, the next request reuses a stale token and Personio returns 401. The auto-reauth path masks this once but still burns a quota slot per cycle — the canonical fix is to honour the rotation. Tests under `tests/test_connector.py::test_token_rotates_on_each_response` and `test_rotated_token_persists_via_set_token` lock the contract.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `client_id` | string | yes | `self.client_id` | OAuth2 client_id (also passed to the HTTP client) |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | `self.client_secret` | OAuth2 client_secret |
| `partner_id` | string | no | `self.partner_id` (default `SHIELVA`) | `X-Personio-Partner-ID` header |
| `app_id` | string | no | `self.app_id` (default `shielva-connector`) | `X-Personio-App-ID` header |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (default `https://api.personio.de/v1`) | Override for sandbox / proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["client_id", "client_secret"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_PERSONIO_BASE = "https://api.personio.de/v1"
_TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS = 24 * 60 * 60
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `PersonioConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::PersonioHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `client_id` + `client_secret`; push them into `PersonioHTTPClient`. Does NOT call Personio.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
Delegates to `authenticate()` so the gateway's OAuth2 hook works uniformly.

### `async authenticate() -> TokenInfo`
`POST /auth?client_id=…&client_secret=…`. Captures the bearer from the `Authorization` response header, falls back to `data.token` in the body. Persists via `BaseConnector.set_token()`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
`GET /company/employees?limit=1`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Pages through `/company/employees` (`limit=200`), normalizes each into `NormalizedDocument`, and ingests via `BaseConnector.ingest_document`. Honours `since` as a `updated_since=` filter when `full=False`.

children.title: Employees
children.content:
### `async list_employees(*, limit=100, offset=0, email=None, updated_since=None) -> Dict`
`GET /company/employees`.

### `async get_employee(employee_id) -> Dict`
`GET /company/employees/{id}`.

### `async create_employee(*, first_name, last_name, email, hire_date, department=None, position=None, extra=None) -> Dict`
`POST /company/employees`. Body wrapped as `{employee: {attributes: {...}}}`.

### `async update_employee(employee_id, attributes) -> Dict`
`PATCH /company/employees/{id}`. Body wrapped as `{employee: {attributes: {...}}}`.

### `async list_custom_attributes() -> Dict`
`GET /company/employees/custom-attributes`.

### `async get_employee_normalized(employee_id) -> NormalizedDocument`
Convenience: fetch + run `normalize_employee` and return the `NormalizedDocument` directly.

children.title: Attendances + Time-Offs
children.content:
### `async list_attendances(*, employees=None, start_date=None, end_date=None, limit=100, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /company/attendances`. `employees` is an array of employee IDs (`employees[]=` query repeats).

### `async create_attendance(*, employee, date, start_time, end_time, break_time=0, comment=None) -> Dict`
`POST /company/attendances`. Body: `{attendances: [{employee, date, start_time, end_time, break, comment?}]}`.

### `async update_attendance(attendance_id, attributes) -> Dict`
`PATCH /company/attendances/{id}`.

### `async list_time_offs(*, start_date=None, end_date=None, limit=100, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /company/time-offs`.

### `async create_time_off(*, employee_id, time_off_type_id, start_date, end_date, half_day_start=False, half_day_end=False) -> Dict`
`POST /company/time-offs`.

### `async list_time_off_types() -> Dict`
`GET /company/time-off-types`.

children.title: Documents · Org structure
children.content:
### `async list_documents(employee_id, category_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /company/document-categories?employee_id=…`.

### `async upload_document(*, employee_id, file_bytes, filename, category_id, title=None) -> Dict`
`POST /company/employees/{id}/documents` — multipart form with the file bytes + `category_id` + optional `title`.

### `async list_departments() -> Dict`
`GET /company/departments`.

### `async list_offices() -> Dict`
`GET /company/offices`.

### `async list_projects() -> Dict`
`GET /company/projects`.

children.title: Recruitment
children.content:
### `async list_applications(*, limit=100, offset=0, status=None) -> Dict`
`GET /recruiting/applications`. `status` filters by ATS state (`in_progress`, `hired`, `rejected`, …).

### `async get_applicant(applicant_id) -> Dict`
`GET /recruiting/applicants/{id}`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
PersonioError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── PersonioAuthError                  # 401 / 403 — token invalid / lacks scope
├── PersonioBadRequestError            # 400
├── PersonioNotFoundError              # 404
├── PersonioConflictError              # 409 — duplicate email etc.
├── PersonioRateLimitError             # 429 — retry_after_s
├── PersonioServerError                # 5xx
└── PersonioNetworkError               # transport (timeout, DNS, reset)
```

Back-compat alias preserved:
```
PersonioNotFound = PersonioNotFoundError    # legacy name from older code
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately (no retry) |
| 401 | Auto re-auth once; on second 401 raise `PersonioAuthError` |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After`; otherwise exponential backoff `min(2 ** attempt + jitter, 32s)`, retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.HTTPError` (timeout, network) | Wrapped in `PersonioNetworkError`; retried by `helpers.utils.with_retry` |

### Token rotation gotcha

Personio rotates the bearer on every successful response. The client owns rotation in `_capture_rotated_token` — if you bypass `PersonioHTTPClient` and build your own request, your second call WILL 401. Don't bypass.

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches typed exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
5xx → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `client_id` or `client_secret` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Personio shows the secret only once at generation — regenerate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Personio — credential lacks scope | In Personio Dashboard → API Credentials, edit the credential and grant Employees/Attendances/TimeOffs/Documents/Recruiting read scopes (whichever surfaces you use). |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` after a long idle period | The cached token aged out AND the auto re-auth retry also failed | Force a `connector.authenticate()` call. If it still fails, the `client_id` is likely revoked — regenerate it. |
| Every other call returns 401 even though credentials are valid | The HTTP client is being bypassed and the rotated `Authorization` response header is being ignored | All requests MUST go through `PersonioHTTPClient`. Do not build a parallel httpx client. |
| `personio.transient_error` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Personio per-minute rate cap) or 5xx | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `get_employee(id)` | Employee belongs to another tenant or has been off-boarded | Verify the ID matches the account that owns the API credential. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'PersonioNotFound'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `PersonioNotFound = PersonioNotFoundError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| `upload_document` returns 415 | Content-Type drift — server expected multipart but got JSON | The connector sends multipart via `httpx`'s `files=` arg. If you see this, do NOT pre-serialize the body — pass raw `bytes`. |
| Recruitment endpoints return 404 | Account does not have the Recruiting module enabled | Enable Recruiting in Personio billing → re-test. |
